papers.title_en: Adaptive Speech-to-Spike Encoding for Spiking Neural Networks
papers.authors: Taharim Rahman Anon, Jakaria Islam Emon
papers.evidence: 新颖的脉冲神经网络架构
papers.tldr: 针对连续语音信号与脉冲神经网络事件驱动处理的不匹配，提出可学习的残差语音到脉冲编码器，与递归LIF联合端到端训练。在GSC-v2基准上达到94.97%准确率，紧凑模型35k参数即达89.8%，超越以往方法。编码器学习任务对齐的脉冲表示提升类别可分性。同时对比直接反馈对齐与代理梯度BPTT，发现直接反馈对齐可达91.5%。
papers.abstract_en: The mismatch between continuous acoustic signals and discrete event-driven processing remains a fundamental bottleneck for neuromorphic speech processing. Current systems typically rely on fixed spike encoders, forcing downstream Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) to compensate for non-adaptive input representations. To address this, we present a learnable residual speech-to-spike encoder jointly trained end-to-end with a Recurrent Leaky Integrate-and-Fire (R-LIF) backbone. We validate this approach on the Google Speech Commands v2 (GSC-v2) benchmark, achieving up to 94.97% accuracy. Notably, the learned encoder remains highly parameter-efficient with a compact 35k-parameter variant that reaches 89.8%, matching or exceeding prior baselines that require an order of magnitude more parameters. Our encoder-focused analysis, including linear probing and gradient-residual inspection, indicates that the encoder does not target faithful signal reconstruction but instead learns task-aligned spike representations that enhance class separability. Finally, we benchmark bio-inspired, hardware-friendly credit assignment by comparing Direct Feedback Alignment (DFA) with surrogate-gradient BPTT under identical architectures and training conditions. We find that DFA reaches 91.5% accuracy, quantifying the performance trade-off of bio-inspired learning rules for modern neuromorphic audio.
